DigitalTopic: Evaluate smcraft (State Machine Craft) toolkit using the Managerial Moment of Truth framework — separating DESIGN (is the specification/architecture adequate?) from EXECUTION (does the implementation match the design?) across all layers

Model.Description: The schema specification: state kinds (normal/final/history), events, transitions (external/internal), actions (code/timerStart/timerStop), parallel regions, composite nesting, object references. 14 validation rules (V001-V014) specified in caishen/rispecs/StateMachineries/60-*.md.

Parser handles .smdf.json and .smdf.xml. Validates V001-V008, V012, V013 (10 of 14 rules). Missing V009 (history in composite), V010 (parallel regions), V011 (parallel nextState), V014 (timer refs). Design is solid but execution has gaps in advanced validation.

Spec 62 defines the code generation contract: SMDF → target language (Python, TypeScript). Visitor pattern over enriched model. Outputs: state enum, base/leaf state classes, context class, feeder classes. Two language targets specified.

Python PythonCodeGenerator produces runnable FSM code — working. TypeScript codegen exists in ts/src/codegen.ts but NOT wired to smcg CLI. MCP server reimplements a lightweight inline codegen instead of using the real package. Three separate codegen implementations, not unified.

Spec 61 defines: State base class, Context/ContextAsync, TransitionHelper, Observer pattern (Null/Console/Logger), timer management, feeder integration. Hierarchical state entry/exit chain semantics specified.

Python runtime working — streetlight test confirms end-to-end. TypeScript runtime built with dist/. No pytest suite, no TS test suite beyond one file. Runtime works but is unverified beyond one example.

Data model supports recursive StateDef.states[] and ParallelDef. Composite states are first-class. MMOT.md names the desire for visual drill-down. But NO specification exists for: the drill-down UX, inner-end → parent-exit linking semantics, breadcrumb navigation, or parallel region visual rendering.

Canvas.tsx collectAllStates() flattens everything to one level. Composite states render with dashed border but clicking does NOT drill in. MCP tools have no concept of 'current depth' or 'navigate into composite'. Zero execution against the desired outcome.

Spec 63 defines: SVG canvas, drag-and-drop states, draw-mode transition wiring, event management, settings panel, validation panel, code preview. Zustand store with undo/redo. Component decomposition is clean (7 components + store).

All 7 components built. Canvas works (drag, wire, event picker, keyboard shortcuts, context menu). Undo/redo with 50 levels. BUT: 'Generate' button only exports JSON (no backend bridge to smcg). No REST/API layer connecting web to Python. Web is an island.
11 tools covering the full design-validate-generate cycle. 1 resource (live definition). 1 prompt (guided design flow). Stdio transport. Tool names are verb-oriented and clear.

All 11 tools compiled and functional. But: in-memory only (no persistence), generate_code uses lightweight inline codegen (not real PythonCodeGenerator), no composite state navigation tools. Functional for flat state machines, inadequate for hierarchical.

Caishen specs 60-63 exist as upstream source (solid design heritage). But smcraft has NO autonomous rispecs — smcraft/rispecs/ was empty until this session. An agent cannot re-implement smcraft from its own documentation.

README.md exists but is informal. KINSHIP.md exists but omits mia-code-server. No RISE-framework specs written. Caishen specs describe the C# origin, not the Python/TS/Web reality. Execution of specification work: not started.

KINSHIP.md describes smcraft as domain-agnostic toolkit. Mentions jgtutils, jgt-data-server, jgt-code as consumers. Does NOT design integration with mia-code-server. Creative process stages (Germination → Assimilation → Completion) as FSM candidates: not specified.

jgt-strategy-api's 13-state FSM was designed using smcraft-derived patterns. But: no mia-code-server integration exists, no shared SMDF definitions for creative workflows, smcraft MCP not registered in any platform's .mcp.json.
